narration: Here's something wild — handing over cash literally triggers pain in your brain. Scientists measured it. And that pain? It's quietly making some people 30% richer.
visual: Dark background. Brain silhouette with glowing pain-signal pulse near the insula region. Stat '30% LESS SPENDING' springs up in amber.

narration: MIT researchers gave people the same item to buy — once with cash, once with a card. Card users paid up to two times more for the exact same thing. Your card turns off your wallet's alarm.
visual: Light background. Two price tags side by side — 'CASH $20' and 'CARD $40'. Card tag glows amber. '2X MORE' badge pops in with spring animation.

narration: When you swipe, your brain never really feels the money leaving. It's abstract. But when you peel bills from your wallet, your insula fires — that financial pain actually works.
visual: Dark background. SVG hand holding a wallet. Bills animate out and stack. Brain icon with radiating amber pulse rings labeled 'PAIN SIGNAL'.

narration: Now do the math. Spend $1,500 a month and cut 30% by using cash — that's $450 freed every month. Invested at 7%, that grows to $31,000 in ten years.
visual: Light background. Bar chart animates: 'CARD $1,500' vs 'CASH $1,050'. Arrow points to piggy bank labeled '$31,000 IN 10 YRS'.

narration: The cash envelope method is old-school on purpose. Divide your budget into labeled envelopes — groceries, dining, fun. When the envelope is empty, you stop. No app, no willpower needed.
visual: Dark background. Three labeled envelopes: 'GROCERIES', 'DINING', 'FUN'. Bills slide into each. One envelope empties and shows a red X.

narration: 93% of transactions are now cashless — almost nobody feels that pain anymore. Use cash for just your top two spending categories and watch your balance grow. Your brain does the work.
visual: Dark background. Person silhouette holding cash on one side, phone on the other. Stat '93% CASHLESS' fades in. CTA text 'CASH = BUILT-IN WILLPOWER' slides up in amber.
